D1 OpticsL3 ApplyReference

Magnifier / Loupe — Virtual Image Formation

Object placed inside focal length of a converging lens; eye sees an upright virtual image at the near point — angular magnification M = 1 + 25 cm / f

Jeweller's loupe: f = 25 mm → M ≈ 11×. Reading magnifier f = 100 mm → M ≈ 3.5×. The "magnifying" trick: image is virtual (formed on same side as object), upright, larger, located near the eye's near point so detail can be resolved.

Slide object distance — keep it inside f for true magnifier behaviour.
